title: Threads 2
description: Sharing mutable data between threads requires synchronization. Mutex provides mutual exclusion.
instructions: Use Arc and Mutex to safely share and modify data across threads.
hints: Mutex::lock() returns a Result wrapping a MutexGuard.
You need to unwrap the Result: counter.lock().unwrap()
The MutexGuard auto-unlocks when dropped (end of scope).
explanation: To share mutable data between threads, combine `Arc<Mutex<T>>`. Arc provides thread-safe shared ownership, while Mutex ensures only one thread can access the data at a time. `lock()` blocks until the lock is acquired, returning a `MutexGuard` that auto-releases the lock when dropped. This pattern prevents data races at compile time.
